steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The cruising speed s varies between 125 mph and 145 mph. 'Between' with stated endpoints means s is at least 125 and at most 145.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: This is a compound inequality: 125 \leq s \leq 145.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: All four answer choices are simple or compound inequalities; only one captures both bounds simultaneously.

explanation:
• Choice A — ‘$s \leq 20$’: 20 = 145 - 125, the range width, but the speed itself is not bounded by 20 mph — it is bounded between 125 and 145. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$s \leq 125$’: Only sets an upper bound of 125, allowing speeds below the minimum of 125 mph, and doesn't capture the maximum of 145. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$s \leq 145$’: Only sets an upper bound of 145, allowing speeds from 0 up to 145, which misses the minimum constraint of 125. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$125 \leq s \leq 145$’: Correctly captures both that the speed is at least 125 mph and at most 145 mph. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
